finding: PRELU_SFPU exists in UnaryOpType enum (line 110) but all SFPU implementation layers were removed in batch nuke commit db3f683e0a5. details: Found ActivationImpl<APPROXIMATION_MODE, ActivationType::Celu> in ckernel_sfpu_activations.h. CELU not wired to compute API or LLK dispatch - only ckernel-level SFPU implementation exists. Uses _calculate_exponential_body_ from ckernel_sfpu_exp.h.
Starting SFPU kernel analysis for expm1 operation
Read ckernel_sfpu_expm1.h - calls calculate_exponential_body_improved then subtracts 1.0f
Non-approx path: sfpu_exp(setsgn(val,0)) -> _sfpu_exp_(val); if val<0: sfpu_reciprocal -> _sfpu_reciprocal_<3>
math_approx_mode=false (default), template approx=false (default). Approx path: bit manipulation trick. Non-approx path: _sfpu_exp_ + _sfpu_reciprocal_<3>

key_findings: PRELU_SFPU enum present, SFPU kernel nuked. Structurally identical _calculate_lrelu_ in ckernel_sfpu_relu.h documented as reference. Raw TTI instruction style with SFPSETCC/SFPMUL/SFPENCC CC pattern.

SFPU kernel exists in ckernel_sfpu_activations.h as ActivationImpl<APPROX, ActivationType::Celu>. Uses _calculate_exponential_body_ for exp computation. NOT wired to compute API or LLK dispatch. Default APPROXIMATION_MODE=false uses Horner exp + Newton-Raphson reciprocal.

details: Analysis written to .claude-analysis/selu-1/expm1_analysis.md. Identified potential init issue: expm1_init<false> sets Prgm registers for exp, not reciprocal.
